edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B (RL) is initialized from the DR-Tulu-SFT-8B checkpoint and continues training with online RL via RLER (GRPO variant with token-level loss aggregation, sample packing, 1-step async tool calls, and KL=0.001).

evidence.excerpt: We then further train DR Tulu (SFT) using RLER to allow it to explore and improve both its tool-use and answer capabilities, aided by the evolving rubrics described above.
evidence.explanation: Paper states the RL stage is initialized from the SFT checkpoint.
The model_name variable is the SFT checkpoint and is passed as --model_name_or_path to grpo_fast.py, identifying the warm-start of the RL run.

evidence.excerpt: base_model: - rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B ... This is the RL checkpoint of DR Tulu, an open deep research agent trained on top of [rl-research/DR-Tulu-SFT-8B].
evidence.explanation: Frontmatter base_model and body text both pin DR-Tulu-SFT-8B as the RL warm-start checkpoint.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B (RL) was RL-trained on the publicly released rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data mixture comprising the long-form prompts and the persistent + evolving search-based rubrics generated during RLER training.

evidence.explanation: Confirms the released RL training data corresponds to DR Tulu's RLER prompt+rubric pool.

evidence.excerpt: We focus exclusively on long-form questions for RL training. Following the same LM-based filtering procedure ... we collect approximately 5K new prompts from SearchArena and OpenScholar.
evidence.explanation: Paper describes the RL prompt curation that constitutes the RL data mixture.
The RL training script's only training dataset is the dr-tulu-rl-data HF dataset, mixed at weight 1.0.

evidence.excerpt: datasets: - rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data ... This model has undergone RL training on [this dataset](https://huggingface.co/datasets/rl-research/dr-tulu-rl-data).
evidence.explanation: Frontmatter datasets field and body sentence both name dr-tulu-rl-data as the RL training corpus.

evidence.excerpt: This dataset contains the RL training data for DR Tulu, containing prompts and search-based rubrics generated from OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts, with rubrics generated using GPT-4.1.
evidence.explanation: Identifies the dataset (the file's own card) as RL training data for DR Tulu, establishing trained_on.
Dataset mixer flag.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: Approximately 5K filtered long-form prompts for RL training were drawn from SearchArena (in addition to OpenScholar), forming part of the RL prompt pool.

evidence.excerpt: we collect approximately 5K new prompts from SearchArena (Miroyan et al., 2025) and OpenScholar (Asai et al., 2024).
evidence.explanation: Paper names SearchArena as a long-form RL prompt source.

evidence.excerpt: prompts and search-grounded rubrics generated from OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts
evidence.explanation: Lattice description names SearchArena prompts as a source; the canonical SearchArena release on HF is lmarena-ai/search-arena-24k.

evidence.excerpt: containing prompts and search-based rubrics generated from OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts
evidence.explanation: Names SearchArena as a prompt source for the RL data DR-Tulu-8B was trained on.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1-mini (gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14) was the LM judge used to score every rollout against each persistent and evolving rubric (returning 0/0.5/1) and to compute the citation reward; its judgement governed inclusion of each rubric's signal in the GRPO reward, hence filtered_by.

evidence.excerpt: For rubric and citation scoring, we use GPT-4.1-mini (gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14) as an LM judge
evidence.explanation: Paper names GPT-4.1-mini as the LM judge that scores rollouts against rubrics for the RL reward.
RUBRIC_JUDGE_MODEL is the env var read by the RLER reward to invoke the LM rubric judge; gpt-4.1-mini is the global default judge for DR-Tulu-8B's RL run.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1 served as the rubric generator G_rubric: it produced both the persistent initial search-based rubrics (after retrieving web/paper evidence per prompt) and the evolving positive/negative rubrics that compare current-policy rollouts during training; these LM-generated rubrics constitute the reward signal.

evidence.excerpt: we use GPT-4.1 as an rubric generator for both initial and evolving rubrics.
evidence.explanation: Paper names GPT-4.1 as the rubric generator that produces the persistent and evolving rubrics defining the RL reward.

evidence.excerpt: search-grounded rubrics generated from OpenScholar and SearchArena prompts (rubrics generated with GPT-4.1) used during RLER training of DR-Tulu-8B
evidence.explanation: The lattice description identifies GPT-4.1 as the model that generated the rubric content embedded in the rl-data dataset.
Names GPT-4.1 as the model that generated the search-based rubrics included in the RL training data.
GPT-4.1 generates the rubrics.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: AstaBench-ScholarQA-CS2 (SQAv2) was used as a long-form scientific-literature-synthesis benchmark with rubric, answer-quality, citation-precision, and citation-recall metrics; DR Tulu-8B (RL) achieved 86.8 (rubric 84.8, Cite-P 90.6, Cite-R 76.1).

evidence.excerpt: DR Tulu-8B (RL) ... 86.8 ... AstaBench-ScholarQA-CS2 (SQAv2) ... Rubric Answer Cite-P Cite-R
evidence.explanation: Tables 2 and 3 report DR Tulu-8B (RL) scores on AstaBench-ScholarQA-CS2.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: HealthBench evaluates healthcare deep-research with physician-annotated rubrics; DR Tulu-8B (RL) scored 50.2 (negative rubrics allowed).

evidence.explanation: Table 2 reports DR Tulu-8B (RL) on HealthBench.
OpenAI's healthbench oss_eval/hard/consensus URLs are the eval source.
Eval table column HealthBench with score 52.8 documents this benchmark for DR-Tulu-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: ResearchQA evaluates synthesis over up-to-date scientific literature across 75 fields; DR Tulu-8B (RL) scored 74.3.

evidence.explanation: Table 2 reports DR Tulu-8B (RL) on ResearchQA.
Identifies realliyifei/ResearchQA as the eval source.

evidence.excerpt: | Benchmark | SQAv2 | HealthBench | ResearchQA | ... | DR-Tulu-8B (this model) | 88.3 | 52.8 | 75.7 |
evidence.explanation: Eval table column ResearchQA with score 75.7 documents this benchmark for DR-Tulu-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DeepResearchBench (DRB) covers general-domain PhD-level research tasks; DR Tulu-8B (RL) scored 43.4 with per-aspect scores Comprehensiveness 41.7, Depth 41.8, Instruction 48.2, Readability 41.3.

evidence.excerpt: DR Tulu-8B (RL) ... DeepResearchBench (DRB) Comp Depth Instruction Readability ... 41.7 41.8 48.2 41.3
evidence.explanation: Tables 2 and 3 report DR Tulu-8B (RL) per-aspect scores on DeepResearchBench.
Eval table column DeepResearch Bench with score 45.4 documents this benchmark for DR-Tulu-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: SimpleQA was used as a short-form factuality benchmark (top-1 prediction, GPT-4.1 judge); DR Tulu-8B (RL) scored 80.1.

evidence.excerpt: DR Tulu-8B (RL) ... SimpleQA 80.1 ... Our short-form evaluation suite comprises SimpleQA (Wei et al., 2024)
evidence.explanation: Section 6.1 names SimpleQA and Table 5 reports the DR Tulu-8B (RL) score.

evidence.excerpt: SimpleQA: Measuring short-form factuality in large language models
evidence.explanation: The eval script targets the SimpleQA test set.
Eval table column SimpleQA with score 75.9 documents this benchmark for DR-Tulu-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: 2Wiki (2WikiMultihopQA) was used as a multi-hop short-form benchmark (top-1 prediction, GPT-4.1 judge); DR Tulu-8B (RL) scored 68.0.

evidence.explanation: Section 6.1 names 2Wiki and Table 5 reports the score.
Eval table column 2Wiki with score 68.9 documents this benchmark for DR-Tulu-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: WebWalkerQA was used as a short-form web-navigation QA benchmark (top-1 prediction, GPT-4.1 judge); DR Tulu-8B (RL) scored 39.1.

evidence.excerpt: DR Tulu-8B (RL) ... WebWalker 39.1 ... WebWalkerQA (Wu et al., 2025a)
evidence.explanation: Section 6.1 names WebWalkerQA and Table 5 reports the score.
Eval table column WebWalker with score 39.0 documents this benchmark for DR-Tulu-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1 served as the LLM judge across all DR Tulu-8B (RL) evaluations: long-form rubric scoring, citation scoring, GeneticDiseasesQA scoring, and short-form top-1 correctness scoring.

evidence.excerpt: Outputs are scored with GPT-4.1 along several dimensions ... evaluated with GPT-4.1 as the LLM judge under a unified evaluation pipeline
evidence.explanation: Paper specifies GPT-4.1 is the LM judge for both GeneticDiseasesQA and the short-form QA evaluations.
evaluate.py defaults grader_model to gpt-4.1-2025-04-14 for the HealthBench branch.
evaluate.py grader default and BrowseComp branch point to gpt-4.1 for grading.
evaluate.py dispatches simpleqa to SimpleQAEval with the default gpt-4.1 grader.
evaluate.py default grader is gpt-4.1 for the short-form judge metric used by dsqa.
Default grader for the genetic-diseases QA branch.

evidence.excerpt: Judge whether the following [response] to [question] is correct based on the precise and unambiguous [correct_answer]. ... correct: yes or no
evidence.explanation: Short-form judge metric uses an LM grader (default gpt-4.1) to label correctness.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-SFT-8B is the team's own SFT-only intermediate checkpoint used as the RLER ablation comparison: holding tool stack and base data fixed, the RL run gains rubric coverage +8.2, answer precision +4.4, comprehensiveness +5.4, depth +6.5, citation precision +23.3 and citation recall +22.1 over the SFT checkpoint.

evidence.excerpt: Comparing DR Tulu (SFT) and DR Tulu (RL), we observe consistent gains from RLER across multiple aspects, including rubric coverage (+8.2 points), answer precision (+4.4), comprehensiveness (+5.4), and depth of response (+6.5).
evidence.explanation: Paper presents the SFT-vs-RL comparison as the team's RLER ablation.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: Qwen3-8B with the dr-agent-lib search stack ('Qwen3-8B + Our Search') is used as the no-DR-training ablation baseline: holding the inference pipeline fixed at the DR Tulu agent stack but skipping both SFT and RLER, isolating the contribution of the DR Tulu training recipe. DR Tulu-8B (RL) outperforms Qwen3-8B + Our Search by 31.8 average points on the long-form benchmark suite.

evidence.explanation: Tables 2 and 3 report the Qwen3-8B + Our Search ablation row alongside the DR Tulu-8B (RL) result.

evidence.excerpt: we also evaluated Qwen3-8B and QwQ-32B with naive RAG inference and separately with our inference pipeline using our dr-agent-lib.
evidence.explanation: Paper documents Qwen3-8B as evaluated with the team's own dr-agent-lib pipeline, serving as the no-training ablation.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The RLER reward design (per-question rubrics graded by an LM judge) was inspired by the ScholarQA-CS line of rubric-based long-form benchmarks; RLER extends this rubric-grading paradigm with search-grounded persistent rubrics and policy-co-evolving rubrics during online RL.

evidence.excerpt: Inspired by the success of evaluating long-form responses with detailed rubrics, including our ScholarQA-CS line of benchmarks, we address this with RLER.
evidence.explanation: Blog directly credits the ScholarQA-CS rubric-evaluation paradigm as RLER's inspiration.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B's Ai2-deployed RLER run additionally trains on a converted (long-form rubric-formatted) version of anisha2102/RaR-Medicine-20k-o3-mini at sample-count 3000 in the dataset_mixer_list; convert_rar_data.py reformats RaR's per-question rubrics into the rl-rag long_form schema before push_to_hub as rl-rag/RaR-Medicine-20k-o3-mini-converted.

evidence.explanation: The conversion script identifies anisha2102/RaR-Medicine-20k-o3-mini as the upstream source of the RaR-Medicine mix used in the Ai2 production RL run.

evidence.excerpt: 2. anisha2102/RaR-Medicine-20k-o3-mini ... the first 1000 samples from RaR-Medicine
evidence.explanation: Source explicitly names anisha2102/RaR-Medicine-20k-o3-mini as an RL training data source for DR Tulu and gives the 1000-sample slice.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B's Ai2-deployed RLER run additionally trains on a converted version of anisha2102/RaR-Science-20k-o3-mini at sample-count 1000 in the dataset_mixer_list; convert_rar_data.py performs the schema reformatting prior to inclusion as rl-rag/RaR-Science-20k-o3-mini-converted.

evidence.explanation: The conversion script identifies anisha2102/RaR-Science-20k-o3-mini as the upstream source of the RaR-Science mix used in the Ai2 production RL run.

evidence.excerpt: For the RaR data, we use data from: 1. anisha2102/RaR-Science-20k-o3-mini ... We sample the first 3000 samples from RaR-Science
evidence.explanation: Source explicitly names anisha2102/RaR-Science-20k-o3-mini as an RL training data source for DR Tulu and gives the 3000-sample slice.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on the BrowseComp short-form browsing benchmark (browse_comp_test_set.csv); decryption of problem and answer columns is done with the canary key per OpenAI's release.

evidence.excerpt: BrowseComp: A Simple Yet Challenging Benchmark for Browsing Agents
evidence.explanation: Eval script's docstring identifies BrowseComp as the test set.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rl-research/webwalker_test (WebWalker held-out test split); listed in SUPPORTED_TASKS and explicitly run in both the README per-task loop and the SLURM webwalker_trained script.

evidence.explanation: WebWalker test mapped to rl-research/webwalker_test.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The SLURM webwalker eval scripts invoke evaluate.py with --grader-model gpt-4o, using gpt-4o as the LM judge for DR-Tulu-8B's WebWalker outputs.

evidence.explanation: Sets gpt-4o as the WebWalker grader.
Sets gpt-4o as the WebShaper grader.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rl-research/webshaper-heldout, a 246-example heldout subset of the WebShaper benchmark (Alibaba-NLP/WebShaper); ShortFormQAEval runs it with judge-metric scoring.

evidence.explanation: Identifies WebShaper heldout as the eval source for DR-Tulu's WebShaper task.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rl-research/dsqa, a HF rehost of DeepMind/deepsearchqa with answer/answer_type-aware parsing; ShortFormQAEval grades responses with the judge metric.

evidence.explanation: Maps dsqa task to rl-research/dsqa.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: rl-research/researchqa_official_subset_ids is loaded via hf_hub_download to restrict the ResearchQA test split to the official subset ids used to score DR-Tulu-8B.

evidence.explanation: The official-subset id list filters the ResearchQA test set.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14 is the hardcoded LM grader for ResearchQA in evaluate.py; it scores per-rubric coverage of DR-Tulu-8B's responses (Not at all / Barely / Moderately / Mostly / Completely buckets).

evidence.explanation: evaluate.py uses gpt-4.1-mini for ResearchQA grading.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rl-research/genetic_diseases_qa (47 clinical-genetics questions), a clinical-genetics QA benchmark with rubric+citation grading. Listed in SUPPORTED_TASKS and run in agent/scripts/auto_search.sh's task loop.

evidence.explanation: Identifies the genetic_diseases_qa eval source.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rl-research/deep_research_bench_eval (100 prompts: 50 EN + 50 ZH) for both RACE and FACT metrics on long-form research reports.

evidence.explanation: DRB test split is loaded as the eval target.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: The DRB run_eval.py auto-downloads query.jsonl, criteria.jsonl, and reference.jsonl from rl-research/dr-tulu-eval-data (deep_research_bench/ subdir) to score DR-Tulu-8B; the same eval-data repo also hosts SQA-CS-V2 reference data.

evidence.explanation: DRB criteria/reference files are pulled from rl-research/dr-tulu-eval-data.

evidence.excerpt: Example DR-Tulu outputs and their ASTA-format conversions are available at rl-research/dr-tulu-eval-data under sqa/.
evidence.explanation: Documents that the eval-data repo hosts the SQA evaluation reference outputs for DR-Tulu-8B.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: gemini-2.5-pro is the RACE scorer (Reference-based Adaptive Criteria-driven Evaluation with Dynamic Weighting) for DR-Tulu-8B's Deep Research Bench article quality.

evidence.explanation: RACE_MODEL is gemini-2.5-pro.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: gemini-2.5-flash is the FACT scorer (Factual Abundance and Citation Trustworthiness) for DR-Tulu-8B's Deep Research Bench citations.

evidence.explanation: FACT_MODEL is gemini-2.5-flash.
Default scorer for SQA evaluation.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on the SQA (ScholarQA-CS-v2) sub-task of allenai/asta-bench (tasks/sqa/rubrics_v2_recomputed.json), measuring ingredient_recall, answer_precision, citation_recall, citation_precision, and global_avg.

evidence.explanation: Identifies the asta-bench SQA rubrics_v2 subset as the eval target.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B can be evaluated on the rulins/2wiki_rlvr_no_prompt RLVR formatted 2WikiMultihopQA split via ShortFormQAEval (task: 2wiki_rlvr_no_prompt).

evidence.explanation: Maps 2wiki_rlvr_no_prompt task to the rulins HF dataset.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rulins/tqa_rlvr_no_prompt (TriviaQA in RLVR-no-prompt format) via ShortFormQAEval; also referenced as the 'tqa' SUPPORTED_TASKS alias.

evidence.explanation: Maps tqa to the rulins TriviaQA RLVR dataset.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rulins/nq_rlvr_no_prompt (NaturalQuestions in RLVR-no-prompt format) via ShortFormQAEval (tasks: nq, nq_rlvr_no_prompt).

evidence.explanation: Maps nq to rulins NaturalQuestions RLVR.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rulins/hotpotqa_rlvr_no_prompt (HotpotQA in RLVR-no-prompt format) via ShortFormQAEval.

evidence.explanation: Maps hotpotqa to the rulins HotpotQA RLVR dataset.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rl-rag/gpqa_diamond_rlvr_no_prompt (GPQA-Diamond in RLVR-no-prompt format); ShortFormQAEval forces the exact-match metric for this task because answers are MCQ letters.

evidence.explanation: Maps GPQA tasks to the rl-rag dataset and uses exact-match grading.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rl-rag/hle_rlvr_no_prompt (Humanity's Last Exam in RLVR-no-prompt format) via the 'hle' task in ShortFormQAEval.

evidence.explanation: Maps hle task to the rl-rag HLE RLVR dataset.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rl-rag/gaia_dev (a rl-rag rehost of the GAIA-dev development split) via the 'gaia' task in ShortFormQAEval. (Free-text mention; the dataset is below the lattice's score-50 threshold and is recorded with its literal HF id.)

evidence.explanation: Maps gaia task to rl-rag/gaia_dev.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rl-rag/bc_synthetic_v_2 (BrowseComp-style synthetic prompts v2) via the bc_synthetic_v_2 task in ShortFormQAEval.

evidence.explanation: Maps bc_synthetic_v_2 task to rl-rag/bc_synthetic_v_2.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rl-rag/verifiable_synthetic_depth_one_v2_verified (single-hop verifiable QA with supporting URLs) via ShortFormQAEval.

evidence.explanation: Maps the depth-one verifiable BC synthetic task.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on rl-rag/verifiable_synthetic_varied_depth_o3_verified (varied-depth verifiable QA with answers verified via OpenAI o3) via ShortFormQAEval.

evidence.explanation: Maps the varied-depth verifiable BC synthetic task.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: Although used here for evaluation, the rl-rag/verifiable_synthetic_varied_depth_o3_verified eval set itself was constructed with OpenAI o3 verifying the answers (per its lattice description and the o3-verified suffix in its name); recording this as an indirect generated_by edge documents that the eval set's labels were produced by o3.

evidence.excerpt: Synthetic varied-depth verifiable QA prompts published by rl-rag, with answers verified via OpenAI o3
evidence.explanation: Lattice description states that o3 verified the eval set's answers.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR-Tulu-8B is evaluated on a 1000-question random subset of 2WikiMultihopQA released by Akari Asai (akariasai/2wiki_rand1k); responses are graded by a judge LM via ShortFormQAEval.

evidence.explanation: Maps the 2wiki task to the akariasai/2wiki_rand1k HF dataset.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: DR Tulu-No-RLER-8B is the team's own design-space ablation companion to DR-Tulu-8B (same SFT base, same RL data, RLER removed) used to measure the contribution of the evolving-rubrics reward component.

evidence.excerpt: This is an ablation model! ... Our real model is rl-research/DR-Tulu-8B. ... This model is trained without RLER, and is an ablation model for analysing the effect of RLER.
evidence.explanation: Card explicitly designates this checkpoint as the ablation companion to DR-Tulu-8B for studying RLER's contribution.

edges.subject: rl-research/dr-tulu-8b
edges.description: GPT-4.1 acts as the rubric judge that scores responses against evolving rubrics; this judgment determines reward and is therefore an inclusion/scoring filter.

evidence.explanation: GPT-4.1 is the rubric judge.
